Title: Re-examining the role of the glyoxylate cycle in oilseeds

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ecotype Columbia; mutant alleles icl-2, ped1-3 mentioned
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintain or enhance glyoxylate-cycle capacity when engineering seed oil composition or reserve remobilization, especially for poor-light establishment and likely in endospermic oilseeds. Avoid disrupting core lipid mobilization genes because seedling establishment depends on reserve use. Could be paired with modified oil traits only if post-germinative carbon remobilization remains functional.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Fatty acid β-oxidation is essential for seedling establishment; disruption blocks normal post-germinative growth, showing lipid reserves are indispensable even when glyoxylate-cycle mutants can survive under favorable light.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis of Arabidopsis ped1-3 during germination and seedling establishment; physiological comparison with wild type and glyoxylate-cycle mutants.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Dark growth; supplementary exogenous sugars; glasshouse growth under 16 h light at 20°C; suboptimal/prolonged low-light conditions discussed for reserve use.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Genes encoding fatty acid β-oxidation enzymes are coordinately induced during germination and post-germinative growth with glyoxylate-cycle genes.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: None reported; metabolite profiling proposed for future work but not presented
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PED1 encodes 3-ketoacyl-CoA thiolase and is cited as evidence that β-oxidation, unlike the glyoxylate cycle, is indispensable in Arabidopsis seedling establishment.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function arrests seedling development immediately after germination.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PED1-dependent β-oxidation is essential for Arabidopsis seedling establishment. 2. ped1 mutants arrest after germination, unlike icl/mls mutants that can establish under favorable light. 3. This demonstrates that lipid reserves are required and that glyoxylate-cycle redundancy depends on continued respiratory use of lipids.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Soluble carbohydrate from storage lipid remobilization
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides carbon and energy for early seedling growth and establishment

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Glyoxylate cycle linked to lipid-to-carbohydrate conversion
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preserve ICL activity when modifying seed oils to ensure efficient post-germinative reserve mobilization, especially under suboptimal light and likely in endospermic oilseeds. May be a target to tune seedling vigor rather than seed composition directly.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves isocitrate to glyoxylate and succinate, bypassing the CO2-releasing steps of the TCA cycle.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for net conversion of acetate/storage lipid-derived carbon to carbohydrate; icl mutants cannot convert acetate to carbohydrate and show reduced survival under low-light or prolonged-dark post-germinative conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Arabidopsis loss-of-function mutant analysis; enzyme activity assays; mRNA accumulation during post-germinative growth; physiological rescue with exogenous sugars; prior in vitro flux studies in castor bean endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms precursor succinate for gluconeogenesis and anaplerosis from acetyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Germination/post-germinative growth; darkness strongly inhibits lipid breakdown/growth in mutants; exogenous sugar rescues growth; suboptimal light reduces establishment; induction also reported with carbohydrate deprivation, shading, defoliation, senescence, pollen development, embryogenesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High during Arabidopsis post-germinative growth coincident with lipid breakdown; also induced in pollen, embryos, senescing cotyledons/leaves, and carbohydrate-deprived tissues in other species.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Arabidopsis germinating seedlings; also broadly in oilseed germination and other carbon-starved tissues
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MLS; glyoxysomal malate dehydrogenase; citrate synthase; aconitase; PEP carboxykinase
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed glyoxylate-cycle bypass step; branch point bypassing decarboxylative TCA reactions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Arabidopsis contains a single-copy ICL gene. In vitro castor bean studies suggest ICL helps control flux from acetate to sugar. Pathway is nonessential for Arabidopsis establishment under favorable light but important for stress resilience during establishment.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes inability to synthesize carbohydrate from acetate, reduced hypocotyl growth in dark, and reduced establishment under suboptimal light/prolonged darkness.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as an important control point for flux from acetate to sugar in castor bean; key control step in glyoxylate-cycle function
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ICL is essential for net conversion of lipid-derived acetyl-CoA/acetate into carbohydrate in Arabidopsis seedlings. 2. icl mutants still establish under favorable light because lipids can be respired, but they fail under prolonged darkness or suboptimal light. 3. ICL therefore supports both gluconeogenesis and anaplerotic metabolism during oilseed establishment.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports gluconeogenesis, anaplerosis, and seedling establishment from storage oils

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Glyoxylate cycle linked to lipid-to-carbohydrate conversion
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Retain MLS when engineering seed reserve composition, because it functions with ICL in post-germinative reserve remobilization and likely contributes to stress-robust establishment.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses glyoxylate with acetyl-CoA to form malate in the glyoxylate cycle.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MLS is required for a functional glyoxylate cycle; Arabidopsis mls mutants lack MLS activity yet can establish under favorable conditions, indicating pathway redundancy in permissive environments but physiological importance for reserve mobilization.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Arabidopsis loss-of-function mutant analysis; enzyme activity and mRNA accumulation during post-germinative growth.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Produces malate that feeds gluconeogenesis and supports carbon transfer/anaplerosis from lipid-derived acetyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Germination/post-germinative growth; expression induced by carbohydrate deprivation, shading/defoliation in cucumber, sucrose withdrawal, senescence, pollen development, embryogenesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High during Arabidopsis post-germinative growth in parallel with ICL and lipid loss.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Arabidopsis germinating seedlings; also induced in other species during germination and carbon starvation
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ICL; glyoxysomal malate dehydrogenase; citrate synthase
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Core glyoxylate-cycle bypass step downstream of ICL
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Arabidopsis contains a single-copy MLS gene. Promoter deletion studies in cucumber identified distinct elements required for induction during germination and altered carbohydrate status.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss abolishes MLS activity and may impair efficient reserve remobilization, though visible establishment defects are limited under favorable conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key pathway enzyme; rate-limiting role not established in this review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MLS is co-induced with ICL during post-germinative lipid breakdown. 2. Arabidopsis mls mutants demonstrate that the glyoxylate cycle can be dispensable for visible establishment under favorable conditions. 3. MLS nevertheless remains a core enzyme enabling lipid-derived carbon to enter gluconeogenic/anaplerotic metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports carbohydrate synthesis and early seedling growth from storage lipids

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve conversion of lipid-derived carbon into soluble sugars during seedling establishment or when engineering unusual seed oils; should be coordinated with glyoxylate-cycle and β-oxidation capacity.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts oxaloacetate to phosphoenolpyruvate, enabling gluconeogenic synthesis of carbohydrate from lipid-derived carbon.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides the gluconeogenic step that channels glyoxylate-cycle-derived carbon into soluble carbohydrates during oilseed post-germinative growth.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway assignment from metabolic scheme in the review; cited co-ordinate induction of gluconeogenic genes with glyoxylate-cycle genes during germination.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate gluconeogenic precursor leading to soluble carbohydrate synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Genes encoding gluconeogenic enzymes are co-induced with glyoxylate-cycle genes during Arabidopsis post-germinative growth.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Cytosolic malate dehydrogenase; glyoxylate-cycle enzymes upstream
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as a required step connecting glyoxylate-cycle output to soluble carbohydrate production; no mutant analysis provided in this review.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PEP carboxykinase links glyoxylate-cycle-derived oxaloacetate to gluconeogenesis. 2. Its expression is coordinately induced with glyoxylate-cycle genes during reserve mobilization. 3. It is part of the pathway converting seed oil carbon into soluble carbohydrate.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides transportable carbon and biosynthetic substrate during early establishment

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Glyoxysomal TCA/glyoxylate-cycle support and β-oxidation redox balancing
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to improve reserve mobilization efficiency and β-oxidation redox balance in engineered oilseeds; future mutant analysis suggested by authors.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Interconverts malate and oxaloacetate with NAD+/NADH, potentially regenerating NAD+ required for glyoxylate-cycle function and fatty acid β-oxidation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to play a central role in balancing cofactors for the glyoxylate cycle and β-oxidation; by analogy with yeast peroxisomal malate dehydrogenase, impairment may restrict fatty acid breakdown.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical pathway analysis and comparison with yeast mutant evidence; no Arabidopsis mutant validation in this review.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supports precursor supply and redox recycling required for lipid-derived carbon conversion
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors specifically propose isolating Arabidopsis mutants to test its role in NAD+ regeneration for the glyoxylate cycle and β-oxidation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Predicted impairment of fatty acid β-oxidation and reserve use if disrupted.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Oxaloacetate or malate depending on direction
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Central supportive step proposed; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Glyoxysomal malate dehydrogenase is proposed to regenerate NAD+ for both the glyoxylate cycle and β-oxidation. 2. Its function may be mediated through a malate–aspartate shuttle. 3. Genetic proof in plants was highlighted as an important future need.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports reserve mobilization from seed lipids

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Entry step to glyoxylate cycle/TCA interface during lipid mobilization
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential diagnostic target to distinguish citrate-shuttle versus acetyl-carnitine-shuttle models of lipid-derived respiration; preserve function in seed-oil engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses oxaloacetate with acetyl-CoA to form citrate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed glyoxysomal citrate synthase could be required if citrate export mediates transfer of lipid-derived carbon to mitochondria for respiration in Arabidopsis seedlings.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway inference and future genetic test proposed by authors; no direct mutant analysis in this review.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies citrate as an intermediate for carbon transfer toward respiration and glyoxylate-cycle operation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors propose that mutants in glyoxysomal citrate synthase would clarify whether Arabidopsis uses a citrate/oxaloacetate shuttle rather than an acetyl-carnitine shuttle to respire lipid-derived carbon.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disruption could impair respiratory use of lipid-derived carbon if citrate shuttle model is correct.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Glyoxysomal citrate synthase is a candidate determinant of how lipid-derived carbon is transferred for respiration. 2. Its importance depends on whether a citrate/oxaloacetate shuttle operates in Arabidopsis. 3. The review identifies it as a priority target for future mutant analysis.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ICL and MLS promoters contain separate conserved sequence elements required for germination induction and altered-carbohydrate-status induction in cucumber; promoter deletion analysis reported. No specific promoter name given.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Hexokinase-linked sugar sensing could be exploited to derepress glyoxylate-cycle genes under carbohydrate limitation, potentially improving reserve remobilization or stress recovery; however, broad sugar-signaling effects warrant caution.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a sugar-sensing regulator implicated in repression of ICL and MLS expression by hexoses; removal of sucrose or carbohydrate deprivation induces glyoxylate-cycle gene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Sugar feeding and sugar-analog experiments in cucumber cell cultures and mesophyll protoplasts; induction/repression assays of ICL and MLS activities/expression; promoter deletion analysis of downstream genes.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller linking carbohydrate status to glyoxylate-cycle activation, thereby influencing lipid-to-carbohydrate conversion under carbon starvation.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sucrose withdrawal; feeding hexose sugars and sugar analogues; shading or defoliation of shoots; exogenous sucrose blocks induction in detached cotyledons and roots.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not reported for the regulator itself
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Metabolite-dependent repression by sugar status is implicated
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional control of glyoxylate-cycle gene expression in response to carbohydrate status
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This review implicates hexokinase in sugar-mediated repression but does not identify a specific hexokinase isoform or provide direct Arabidopsis genetics.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulating sugar sensing may broadly perturb growth and carbon allocation.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses ICL and MLS expression in response to sugars/hexose sensing
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Hexokinase is implicated in sugar-mediated repression of glyoxylate-cycle genes. 2. Carbohydrate deprivation induces ICL and MLS, whereas exogenous sugars suppress their induction. 3. This places sugar sensing upstream of lipid-to-carbohydrate remobilization programs.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Germinating tissues, roots, cotyledons under carbohydrate deprivation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports adaptive reserve remobilization during carbon starvation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Hexose sugars; sucrose status; shading; defoliation; carbohydrate deprivation
